title: rubus scaber var. subcanus
intro: Rubus scaber var. subcanus, also known as the thimbleberry, is a type of deciduous shrub that belongs to the rose family and is native to East Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it effective in treating various health issues such as fever, sore throats, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Rubus scaber var. subcanus include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infusio, or dried and made into capsules for consumption.
